title: Cosmos Sticky Stack — pure-CSS sticky-scroll deck reveal
summary: A pure-CSS sticky scroll: six themed slides each use position:sticky pinned at a slightly larger top offset, so scrolling stacks them like a deck — each slide rises and covers the previous while the previous one's edge peeks above. The reveal needs NO JavaScript; the six Cosmos themes (Phantom/Solaris/Sacred/Aurora/Cosmic/Nebula) scroll past as full-bleed cards. Runs offline.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html (no server/CDN); keep cosmos-logo.png beside it. HOW IT WORKS: each slide sits in a 100vh .sec and is position:sticky; top:calc(34px + i*16px). Because each later slide pins a little lower and comes later in the DOM, scrolling makes slide i+1 climb up and overlap slide i (rounded corners + shadow show the stack). That's the whole effect — pure CSS. INTERACTION: just scroll. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 exposes window.__seek(t) which sets scrollTop along an eased PING-PONG curve (0 -> bottom -> 0) so the GIF loops seamlessly; capture.cjs steps it WITHOUT the puppeteer `clip` option. TUNE: SLIDES[], slide height/offset, radius. BRAND: each slide uses one of the six theme accents over Phantom dark.
